title: Pass 5.B.4: ferrotorch-jit fusion.rs apply_fused CPU-output silent demote
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-jit audit, GPU_CPU_MISMATCH bucket (1 of 2).

src/fusion.rs:1081-1085 vs docstring at 1075-1080 — `apply_fused` doc says 'produces a new tensor on the same device' and 'When a CUDA device is available the chain could be dispatched via the PTX kernel'. Implementation: input.data()? (CPU readback) → chain.execute_cpu(data)? → TensorStorage::cpu(result). CUDA-input always silently downgraded to CPU output. The 'could be dispatched via PTX' is aspirational, not a code path.

Also: src/codegen.rs:987-1017 — cuda feature is codegen-only; compile() for GpuCuda/GpuPtx returns JitError::GpuBackendUnavailable regardless of feature. cuda = ["dep:cudarc"] in Cargo.toml:24 implies runtime support that doesn't exist.

Acceptance:
- Either route to PTX kernel when input.is_cuda() (real GPU dispatch), OR
- Document the CPU-fallback explicitly + amend doc to remove the 'same device' claim
- Discriminating test asserting fused-output device matches input device.
